title: Argon Oxygen Decarburization (AOD)
author: JSL EKOS
description: This module explains the Argon Oxygen Decarburization (AOD) process used in stainless steel manufacturing to reduce carbon content while preserving valuable alloying elements such as chromium.
learningObjectives: Understand the purpose of the AOD process.
Learn the working principle of AOD.
Recognize safety precautions during AOD operations.

content.introduction: After melting in the Electric Arc Furnace, molten steel is transferred to the Argon Oxygen Decarburization (AOD) converter for refining. The AOD process removes excess carbon while maintaining the required chromium content, making it one of the most important processes in stainless steel production.
content.purpose: The primary purpose of AOD is to refine molten steel by reducing carbon content, adjusting alloy composition, and improving overall steel quality.
content.workingPrinciple: A controlled mixture of oxygen and argon is blown into the molten steel. Oxygen reacts with carbon to form carbon monoxide gas, while argon dilutes the oxygen, reducing chromium oxidation and improving refining efficiency.
processSteps: Transfer molten steel into the AOD converter.
Transfer refined steel to the Ladle Refining Furnace.
safetyPrecautions: Maintain safe distance from molten steel.
content.summary: The AOD process is essential for producing high-quality stainless steel. Proper control of gas injection, alloy additions, and process parameters ensures the required chemical composition while maximizing chromium recovery.
